step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Achondroplasia follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern. When both parents are affected, each parent has a 50% chance of passing on the mutant FGFR3 allele. The offspring thus have a 25% chance of receiving two normal alleles (normal stature), a 50% chance of being heterozygous (affected), and a 25% chance of being homozygous (usually fatal). Therefore, the chance of having an unaffected baby is 25%.
